title: Definition of slice subcategory wrongly closed under desuspension (breaks the theorem)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the section 'The $\mathcal{O}$-slice filtration', the subcategory $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ is defined as 'the smallest full STABLE subcategory ... closed under all homotopy colimits, retracts, and tensors', and it is asserted to be 'automatically closed under (DE)SUSPENSION, cofibers ..., and extensions'.

This is fatal. A stable subcategory is closed under desuspension by definition, but then the MAIN THEOREM is false:

- For $G=e$: the generators are $\{S^m:m\ge n\}$; closing under desuspension gives ALL of $\mathrm{Sp}$, so $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\mathrm{Sp}$ for every $n$. The criterion $\mathrm{conn}(E)\ge n$ then fails the 'only if' direction (e.g. $E=S^0$, $n=5$). This also CONTRADICTS the converse base case, which claims $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\mathrm{Sp}_{\ge n}$ — and $\mathrm{Sp}_{\ge n}$ is NOT closed under desuspension.
- General $G$: $\Sigma^{-M}N^TS^1\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ would give $r_G\,\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G(\Sigma^{-M}N^TS^1))=r_G(|T/G|-M)\ll n$, directly contradicting Lemma forward.

Fix: $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ must be the smallest full subcategory containing the generators and closed under colimits, extensions, retracts, SUSPENSION (only), and $G/H_+\otimes(-)$ — i.e. the connective half of the slice tower, NOT a stable subcategory. The proof's actual usages (closure under cofibers of maps between objects of the subcategory, colimits, retracts, tensoring with admissible cells) are all compatible with this corrected notion and never need desuspension; but the definition as written is self-contradictory and must be rewritten. Remove the word 'stable' and the '(de)suspension' claim.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
The subcategory $\langle\mathcal G\rangle_{\ge}$ is explicitly defined to be closed under colimits, retracts, orbit tensors, and **positive** suspensions only, with an explicit paragraph stating it is NOT closed under desuspension (otherwise it would collapse to all of $\mathrm{Sp}^G$). So the definition is now correct. This gap is closed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Verification: gap is resolved in current `solution.tex`.

I re-read the section 'The $\mathcal{O}$-slice filtration' and verified the definition is now correct and self-consistent throughout.

**Definition.** $\langle\mathcal G\rangle_{\ge}$ (hence $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$) is defined as the smallest full subcategory of $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ containing the generators and closed under:
- (o1) homotopy colimits, including cofibers of maps *between its objects*, extensions, and **positive** suspensions $\Sigma^\ell$ ($\ell\ge0$) only;
- (o2) retracts;
- (o3) orbit tensors $G/H_+\otimes(-)$.

The word 'stable' is **absent** (grep confirms zero occurrences of 'stable subcategory' in the definition), and there is an explicit paragraph stating the subcategory is **not** closed under desuspension, with the correct justification: closing under $\Omega=\Sigma^{-1}$ would, since the cells generate $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ as a stable category, collapse it to all of $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ and trivialize the filtration.

**Consistency checks across the proof (all confirmed):**
- The $G=e$ / base case ($\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge 0}$): identified with the *connective* $G$-spectra — the connective half, which is genuinely NOT closed under desuspension, exactly as the issue required. No more contradiction with $\mathrm{Sp}_{\ge n}$.
- Forward direction (Lemma forward) and Prop. $\Phi^G(N^TS^1)\simeq S^{|T|}$: no $\Sigma^{-M}N^TS^1$ ever enters the subcategory, so the connectivity lower bound is not violated.
- The nullification/small-object argument and the target-property closure $\mathcal C$ are explicitly stated to require only 'cofibers of maps between objects already in the subcategory, never fibers or desuspensions', and that $\mathrm{Sp}_{\ge m}$ does not possess closure under $\Sigma^{-1}$ — matching the connective notion.
- Remark 'suspension raises slice connectivity' makes only a one-sided containment $\Sigma\,\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge k}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge k+1}$, no desuspension.

All of the proof's actual usages are compatible with the corrected connective notion and never invoke desuspension. The self-contradiction flagged in the issue is gone. (LaTeX compile skipped: no toolchain installed; the change is textual and the surrounding markup is intact.)

Resolving.
